timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Early salt extraction begins at Poiana Slatinei
description: By the 3rd millennium BCE, salt extraction at Poiana Slatinei illustrates some of Europe's earliest known production.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Salt production appears at Solnitsata
description: By the late 3rd millennium BCE, the settlement of Solnitsata was thriving around salt making and exchange.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Egyptians use salt in preservation and ritual
description: Salt became central to preserving food and to mummification practices in ancient Egypt.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Chinese states organize salt production
description: Salt wells and evaporation techniques in ancient China supported one of the earliest state-linked salt industries.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Trans-Saharan salt trade expands
description: Salt moved across Sahara caravan routes and became a strategic commodity in West African exchange networks.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Salted foods spread through Mediterranean trade
description: Growing exchange around the Mediterranean made salt curing a foundational preservation technology.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Greek and Phoenician merchants trade salt widely
description: Classical maritime trade helped make salt a routine commercial staple around the eastern Mediterranean.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Roman salt routes gain importance
description: The later Via Salaria became emblematic of Rome's dependence on salt supply and distribution.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Salt currency traditions persist in Africa and Asia
description: In several regions salt continued to function as a medium of exchange as well as a dietary necessity.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Moorish and Sahelian salt routes intensify
description: Caravan trade linked Saharan mines to the rising commercial cities of West Africa.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Wang Anshi strengthens the Chinese salt monopoly
description: Song fiscal reforms leaned heavily on state revenue from salt, underscoring its role in imperial finance.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Venice prospers through the salt trade
description: Venetian commercial power rested in part on controlling salt markets in the Adriatic and beyond.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Mali's salt-gold exchange reaches a high point
description: Saharan salt remained indispensable to the economy of medieval West Africa and its long-distance trade.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: The French gabelle becomes entrenched
description: The French crown consolidated the gabelle, turning salt taxation into one of the monarchy's most hated fiscal tools.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: British salt taxes shape colonial commerce in India
description: Salt taxation became part of the East India Company's wider fiscal control over South Asia.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Hatred of the gabelle feeds the French Revolution
description: Opposition to unequal salt taxation became part of the broader attack on ancien-regime privilege.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Revolutionary France abolishes the gabelle
description: The National Assembly ended the notorious salt tax during the first phase of the Revolution.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Napoleonic France restores the salt tax
description: The revived gabelle showed how difficult it was for modern states to abandon reliable revenue sources.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Industrial mining deepens the global salt trade
description: Railways and industrial extraction transformed salt from a strategic rarity into a bulk industrial commodity.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Gandhi begins the Salt March
description: Mahatma Gandhi's protest against the British salt tax turned salt into a symbol of mass anticolonial resistance.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Independent India ends the colonial salt tax regime
description: Indian independence removed the salt tax as a major emblem of imperial rule.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Refrigeration reduces salt's role in preservation
description: Mechanical cold storage diminished salt's centuries-old centrality in preserving food.

timeline_description: A global history of salt as mineral, preservative, tax base, trade good, political flashpoint, and everyday seasoning.
title: Salt remains a universal seasoning and industrial input
description: Once a luxury and fiscal weapon, salt persists as an everyday food staple and a major chemical feedstock.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Sericulture emerges in China
description: Chinese traditions place the origins of silk production in the 3rd millennium BCE, making silk one of the world's oldest luxury fibers.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Silk enters courtly culture
description: Early Chinese elites associated silk with status, tribute, and ritual exchange.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Silk weaving becomes established
description: Silk moved from rare court material toward a more developed textile craft with specialized production.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Silk reaches India overland
description: By around 140 BCE, sericulture and silk had spread from China toward India, broadening the geography of silk culture.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: The Silk Road begins to take shape
description: Han expansion into Central Asia opened overland routes that turned silk into a major trans-Eurasian trade good.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Silk becomes known in Rome
description: Roman demand for silk grew rapidly, turning Chinese fabric into a prized import of the classical Mediterranean.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Parthian middlemen profit from silk exchange
description: Intermediate empires along Eurasian routes gained wealth by controlling silk moving between China and the Mediterranean.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: India develops a major silk market
description: By the 2nd century CE India was an important producer, consumer, and transit zone in the silk economy.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Byzantium becomes a key silk consumer
description: Imperial courts in the eastern Mediterranean made silk a marker of political and religious prestige.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Silkworm eggs are smuggled to Byzantium
description: According to tradition, monks carried silkworm eggs from Asia to Constantinople, breaking China's long monopoly.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Islamic realms absorb silk centers
description: The expansion of Islamic rule linked Persian, Central Asian, and Mediterranean silk workshops into new networks.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Italian cities expand silk weaving
description: Cities such as Lucca and Venice became major European centers of silk manufacture and design.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Mongol rule stabilizes Eurasian silk exchange
description: Pax Mongolica temporarily improved long-distance movement of silk, merchants, and techniques.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: European courts intensify silk consumption
description: Silk became deeply embedded in aristocratic fashion, diplomacy, and gift culture across Europe.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Global maritime empires reroute silk commerce
description: Oceanic trade connected Asian silk more directly with European markets and colonial port cities.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: John Kay's flying shuttle accelerates weaving
description: Mechanization in textile production increased the scale and efficiency of cloth manufacture, including silk weaving.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Jacquard loom transforms patterned silk
description: Joseph-Marie Jacquard's loom automated complex designs and reshaped silk weaving in Lyon and beyond.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Meiji Japan modernizes silk production
description: The Meiji state treated silk as a strategic export and invested in modern sericulture and reeling.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Tomioka Silk Mill opens
description: Japan's state-backed Tomioka mill became a landmark in industrial silk production and export modernization.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Rayon challenges silk in mass markets
description: Artificial fibers reduced silk's dominance in many everyday uses while preserving its luxury status.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Nylon reshapes the silk economy
description: Synthetic fibers increasingly replaced silk in products such as stockings and parachutes.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Luxury fashion renews global silk demand
description: High fashion houses continued to treat silk as a prestige material for garments and accessories.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Tomioka Silk Mill gains World Heritage status
description: UNESCO recognition confirmed silk's long importance in industrial and cultural history.

timeline_description: A history of silk from early Chinese sericulture to global luxury fashion and modern industrial production.
title: Silk remains both heritage craft and global luxury fiber
description: Modern sericulture coexists with designer fashion, biotechnology research, and heritage preservation.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Cities improvise isolation during the Plague of Athens
description: Ancient responses to epidemic disease included practical separation of the sick, anticipating later quarantine logic.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Justinianic plague prompts emergency controls
description: Late antique authorities used movement controls and ad hoc isolation as plague spread through the Mediterranean.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Ragusa establishes the trentino
description: The Adriatic port of Ragusa required arrivals from infected places to wait thirty days before entering the city.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Venice opens a permanent lazaretto
description: Venice created one of the first dedicated quarantine islands for isolating ships, goods, and travelers.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: The forty-day quarantina becomes standard
description: The Venetian waiting period of forty days gave quarantine its enduring name and formal shape.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Italian plague ordinances formalize cordons
description: Early modern states combined lazarettos, bills of health, and cordon systems to police epidemic risk.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: London uses household quarantine in the Great Plague
description: Infected houses were shut up and marked as authorities tried to contain urban plague transmission.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Marseille plague revives strict Mediterranean quarantine
description: The plague outbreak at Marseille reinforced the use of cordons and port controls in Europe.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Philadelphia yellow fever drives emergency isolation
description: American cities increasingly adopted quarantine stations and maritime inspection during recurrent epidemics.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Cholera sparks international quarantine disputes
description: Governments struggled to balance commerce and disease control as cholera spread across continents.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: First International Sanitary Conference meets
description: European powers began negotiating shared cross-border disease rules, a precursor to modern health governance.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Immigrant quarantine expands at Ellis Island
description: Inspection and detention became a routine feature of U.S. border health policy in the age of mass migration.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Mary Mallon is first isolated as Typhoid Mary
description: New York forcibly quarantined Mary Mallon as an asymptomatic carrier, creating a landmark public-health controversy.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Influenza closures revive mass quarantine practices
description: Cities worldwide used school closures, gathering bans, and isolation during the deadly influenza pandemic.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: WHO adopts revised International Sanitary Regulations
description: Postwar global health governance standardized disease reporting and travel controls across borders.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: International Health Regulations are first adopted
description: The World Health Assembly replaced older sanitary rules with the IHR framework.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: SARS triggers modern home and travel quarantine
description: The SARS outbreak led governments to revive quarantine, contact tracing, and international travel advisories.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Revised International Health Regulations are adopted
description: The new IHR widened the global legal framework for reporting and responding to public-health emergencies.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Ebola outbreaks bring quarantine back to public debate
description: West African Ebola transmission led to renewed controversy over border checks and mandatory isolation.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: WHO is alerted to pneumonia cases in Wuhan
description: The first official notice that would become the COVID-19 crisis marked the start of a new quarantine era.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Wuhan enters lockdown
description: China's restrictions on movement made quarantine and lockdown central topics in global pandemic response.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: WHO declares COVID-19 a pandemic
description: Governments worldwide expanded quarantines, border controls, and stay-at-home orders after the declaration.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Hotel quarantine becomes a routine border tool
description: Countries such as Australia and New Zealand institutionalized managed quarantine for incoming travelers.

timeline_description: A history of quarantine from medieval plague controls to modern public-health isolation and travel regulation.
title: Quarantine becomes a normalized emergency measure
description: Digital tracing, home isolation, and border screening entrenched quarantine in twenty-first-century governance.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Maize spreads through the Americas

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Rice terraces and irrigation intensify
description: Engineering around grain cultivation reshaped landscapes and labor organization.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Wheat becomes a Mediterranean staple
description: Bread grains anchored exchange and taxation across classical Mediterranean societies.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Columbian Exchange opens grain transfers across oceans
description: After contact between the Old and New Worlds, crops began moving at unprecedented scale between continents.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Maize enters Europe
description: European adoption of maize added a productive New World grain to Old World farming and diets.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Maize spreads into Africa
description: Portuguese-linked maritime trade accelerated maize cultivation across parts of Africa.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Rice cultivation expands in the Atlantic world
description: Rice knowledge, seeds, and labor systems crossed oceans and reshaped plantation zones.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Wheat frontiers move into North America
description: European settler expansion made wheat central to colonial and early national agriculture.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Railroads globalize grain markets
description: Transport networks tied continental interiors to ports and world commodity prices.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Steam milling modernizes flour production
description: Industrial processing changed the scale, quality, and distribution of grain foods.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Hybrid maize transforms yields
description: Seed technology made corn a leading industrial and feed crop in twentieth-century agriculture.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Green Revolution elevates wheat and rice output
description: New varieties, fertilizers, and irrigation sharply raised production in parts of Asia and beyond.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Global trade integrates grain diets further
description: Wheat, rice, and maize became even more globally distributed through liberalized food markets.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Migration of Grains
timeline_description: How wheat, rice, and maize moved across continents and helped shape agriculture, empires, diets, and modern food systems.
title: Three grains dominate the world food system
description: Wheat, rice, and maize remain core to calories, livestock feed, and food security worldwide.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Sea Peoples raid eastern Mediterranean coasts
description: Late Bronze Age maritime raiders became one of the earliest famous examples of organized seaborne predation.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Archaic Greek piracy becomes common
description: In the early Mediterranean world, seaborne raiding often blurred with warfare and trade.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Pompey suppresses Cilician pirates
description: Rome launched a major campaign against Mediterranean piracy, making the issue central to imperial security.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Piracy persists in Roman sea lanes
description: Even after Pompey's campaign, maritime predation remained a recurring challenge to imperial trade.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Viking raiding dominates northern waters
description: Scandinavian seaborne raiders attacked coasts, monasteries, and trade routes from the North Atlantic to rivers inland.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Private raiding grows in the Indian Ocean
description: Commercial wealth and monsoon routes made the Indian Ocean a major theater of piracy and private violence.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Chinese and Japanese coastal piracy intensifies
description: Wokou piracy disrupted East Asian trade and drew strong state responses.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Caribbean piracy emerges with empire
description: Spanish treasure routes and imperial rivalry created ideal conditions for piracy and privateering in the Atlantic.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Francis Drake's raids symbolize licensed piracy
description: English attacks on Spanish treasure blurred the line between piracy and state-backed privateering.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Buccaneers flourish in the Caribbean
description: Semi-legal raiders became central players in imperial competition and maritime violence.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Golden Age of piracy begins
description: As imperial wars ended, unemployed privateers and sailors increasingly turned to unauthorized piracy.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Henry Every's capture shocks world trade
description: The seizure of the Ganj-i-Sawai made piracy a major diplomatic and commercial crisis.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Blackbeard is killed
description: The death of Edward Teach symbolized the growing state offensive against Atlantic pirates.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Bartholomew Roberts is killed
description: The fall of Roberts marked the decline of the classic pirate networks of the Atlantic golden age.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Declaration of Paris condemns privateering
description: Major powers formally renounced privateering, narrowing the legal space that had long fed piracy.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: High seas law begins modern codification
description: The Geneva conventions on the law of the sea helped clarify how states could suppress piracy.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: UNCLOS defines modern piracy law
description: The UN Convention on the Law of the Sea provided the contemporary legal definition of piracy on the high seas.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Digital piracy becomes a mass phenomenon
description: The spread of personal computing and networked media moved piracy from ships to copyrighted files.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Somali piracy spikes in the Gulf of Aden
description: State collapse and busy shipping lanes made the Horn of Africa the focal point of modern maritime piracy.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: International naval patrols intensify
description: Multinational operations sharply raised the costs of piracy off Somalia and protected merchant shipping.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Streaming and torrent piracy challenge media industries
description: Film, music, and software piracy shifted toward websites, apps, and illicit streaming platforms.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: WIPO surveys digital anti-piracy enforcement
description: International policy attention turned to blocking, takedown systems, and cross-border enforcement online.

timeline_description: A long history of piracy from ancient maritime raiding to the law of the sea and digital copyright infringement.
title: Piracy spans both maritime chokepoints and the internet
description: The modern concept now covers attacks on ships and large-scale infringement of digital intellectual property.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of the Map

title: Babylonians make clay tablet maps
description: The earliest known graphic maps show that ancient states were already representing territory visually.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of the Map
timeline_description: How human mapmaking evolved from clay tablets to projections, scientific surveys, satellites, and interactive digital maps.
title: Anaximander is credited with an early world map
description: Greek inquiry began to turn mapping into a rational description of the known world.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of the Map
timeline_description: How human mapmaking evolved from clay tablets to projections, scientific surveys, satellites, and interactive digital maps.
title: Eratosthenes links geography to measurement
description: Hellenistic scholars tied mapping to mathematics and the size of the Earth.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of the Map
timeline_description: How human mapmaking evolved from clay tablets to projections, scientific surveys, satellites, and interactive digital maps.
title: Ptolemy systematizes map coordinates
description: Ptolemy's Geography became a foundational text for latitude, longitude, and projection thinking.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of the Map
timeline_description: How human mapmaking evolved from clay tablets to projections, scientific surveys, satellites, and interactive digital maps.
title: Regional maps circulate in Tang China
description: Chinese mapping traditions developed sophisticated administrative and military uses of maps.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Evolution of the Map
timeline_description: How human mapmaking evolved from clay tablets to projections, scientific surveys, satellites, and interactive digital maps.
title: T and O world maps spread in medieval Europe
